name: hive-mind
description: Visualizes code dependencies as an interactive Obsidian-style graph and provides Copilot tools to analyze change impact across your codebase.

chatParticipants.name: hivemind
chatParticipants.description: Ask about code dependencies, impact analysis, and related files

commands.name: impact
commands.description: Analyze which files are affected if the current file changes

commands.name: deps
commands.description: Show dependencies of the current file

commands.name: cycles
commands.description: Detect circular dependencies in the workspace

disambiguation.description: The user wants to understand code dependencies, imports, file relationships, or how files connect to each other in the codebase.
examples: What files does this file depend on?
What imports does this module have?
Which files are related to this one?

disambiguation.description: The user wants to know which files would be affected by changing a specific file, or wants to understand the downstream impact of a code change.
examples: What would break if I change this file?
Which files depend on this module?
What is the impact of modifying this code?
Show me what files I need to update if I change this

disambiguation.description: The user wants to find circular or cyclic dependencies in the codebase.
examples: Find import cycles in my project

commands.title: Hive Mind: Show Full Code Graph
Hive Mind: Focus Graph on Current File
Hive Mind: Scaffold AI Instructions & Skills
Hive Mind: Invalidate Graph Cache & Reindex
Hive Mind: Show Co-Change Heat Map

hiveMind.maxFiles.description: Maximum number of source files to index. Increase for very large workspaces.

hiveMind.ignoredDirectories.description: Additional directory names to exclude from analysis (on top of the built-in list like node_modules, .git, dist, etc.).

hivemind.clangPath.description: Optional override path to a C/C++ compiler executable used by hivemind_macroExpand and hivemind_buildSubset. Accepts cl.exe (MSVC), g++/gcc, or clang/clang-cl. If empty, Hive Mind auto-detects: cl.exe on Windows, g++ on Linux/Mac, then clang as a fallback. Setting name kept for backwards compatibility.

hiveMind.respectIfdefBranches.description: When true, Hive Mind strips #include directives that lie in dead #ifdef/#if branches based on -D flags from compile_commands.json. Set to false to revert to the legacy behaviour of treating every #include as a real edge (more edges, more false-positive cycles, but no risk of dropping a real edge).

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getDependencies
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns all files that a given source file imports or depends on (direct and transitive). Use this to understand what a file relies on before modifying it.

filePath.description: Path to the file — relative to workspace root or absolute. Fuzzy matching is supported (e.g. just the filename).

depth.description: Traversal depth. 1 = direct only, -1 = all transitive. Defaults to 2.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getImpact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Given a file being modified, returns all other files that import or depend on it — i.e., files that may need to be updated as a result of the change. Always call this when editing a file to check for downstream impact.

filePath.description: Path to the file being changed — relative to workspace root or absolute

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getRelatedFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns all files directly connected to a given file in the dependency graph — both what it imports and what imports it. Use to get a complete picture of a file's role in the codebase.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getFullGraph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the complete dependency graph of the workspace as structured data including hub files, language breakdown, and circular dependencies. Use this to understand overall architecture.

maxNodes.description: Maximum number of nodes to return. Defaults to 150.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_detectCycles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Detects and returns all circular dependency chains in the workspace. Use when investigating build issues, module coupling, or architecture health.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getTestFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Given a source file, returns its associated test files (e.g. foo.test.ts for foo.ts). Given a test file, returns the source file it tests. Use this to find tests that should be updated when modifying source code.

filePath.description: Path to the source or test file

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_findSymbol
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches for a function, class, interface, type, or variable by name across all indexed workspace files. Returns file locations and symbol kinds. Use this to find where a symbol is defined.

symbolName.description: The name of the symbol to search for (function, class, type, variable, etc.)

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_coChanged
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns files that frequently change together with the given file based on git history. These are historically coupled files that may need to be updated together even without direct import relationships.

filePath.description: Path to the file to find co-changed files for

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getContext
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a comprehensive context bundle for a file: its dependencies, dependents, test files, co-changed files, and exported symbols — all in a single call. Use this FIRST when starting work on any file to understand its role and connections. Optionally include the file's source content. This is the recommended starting tool — it replaces calling getDependencies, getImpact, getTestFiles, coChanged, and findSymbol separately.

filePath.description: Path to the file — relative to workspace root or absolute

task.description: Optional description of what you're trying to do. Helps frame the context.

includeContent.description: If true, includes the file's source code (capped at 200 lines). Default false.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_planChange
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Given a file and a description of intended changes, returns a prioritized plan: which files to modify, which to read for context, which tests to run, and which files are peripherally affected. Scores files by structural dependencies, test relationships, and git co-change history. Use this BEFORE making multi-file changes to avoid missing impacted files.

filePath.description: Path to the primary file being changed

description.description: What change is being made — e.g. 'rename the handleRequest method' or 'add a new parameter to the constructor'

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches the entire workspace for text or regex patterns, then ranks results by combining match count with structural signals from the dependency graph. Files whose graph neighbors also match (cluster signal), files near a contextFile, hub files, and files with matching exported symbols are boosted. Use this instead of raw grep when you want the most relevant files first — not just files with the most hits. Supports an optional contextFile to bias results toward a specific area of the codebase.

query.description: The text or regex pattern to search for across all workspace files

isRegex.description: If true, treat query as a regular expression. Default false.

caseSensitive.description: If true, match case. Default false (case-insensitive).

maxResults.description: Maximum number of files to return. Default 30.

contextFile.description: Optional file path to bias results toward. Files structurally connected to this file get boosted in ranking.

includeSnippets.description: If true, include code snippets from top matches. Default true.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_getCppPair
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Given a C/C++ file (.h, .hpp, .cpp, .cc, .inl, .ipp, etc.), returns the matching header/source/inline files by basename match. Use this BEFORE editing any C++ file — modifying a function in foo.cpp almost always requires updating its declaration in foo.h. Skipping this is the #1 cause of broken C++ refactors.

filePath.description: Path to a C/C++ file — relative to workspace root or absolute

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_findMacro
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Locates `#define` macro definitions across all C/C++ files in the workspace. Returns ONLY definitions (not usages) — distinguishes object-like vs function-like macros, shows the body, and reports multiple definitions when present (e.g. platform-gated variants). Use this whenever you encounter a SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE token in C/C++ code and aren't sure if it's a macro, before assuming what it expands to. Critical for codebases like FCIMPL/QCALL/HCIMPL/BEGIN_PINVOKE-style macro-heavy frameworks.

name.description: Macro name to find (e.g. 'FCIMPL3', 'STATIC_CONTRACT_NOTHROW'). By default an exact match — set exact:false for substring search.

exact.description: If true (default), exact name match. If false, substring match — useful when you only know part of the macro name.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of definitions to return. Default 25.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_findReferences
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns every place a C/C++ symbol is referenced. Uses Hive Mind's tree-sitter index — comments, string literals, and dead #ifdef branches are stripped before matching. Each hit is tagged with a `confidence` (high/medium/low) reflecting how unique the name is across the workspace. Use this BEFORE renaming any C/C++ function, class, or variable — for `medium`/`low` confidence results verify each manually. Required: `symbolName`. `filePath` is accepted for compatibility but is not required. Position-only queries (`line`/`character`) are not supported in this build.

filePath.description: Path to a C/C++ file. Accepted for backwards-compatibility but not required — the tree-sitter resolver searches the entire workspace index.

symbolName.description: Name of the symbol to find references for (e.g. 'doFoo', 'MyClass::bar', 'kMaxSize'). Required — position-only lookup is not supported in this build.

line.description: Accepted for backwards-compatibility; ignored by the tree-sitter resolver.

character.description: Accepted for backwards-compatibility; ignored by the tree-sitter resolver.

includeDeclaration.description: If true (default), include the declaration site in results. Set false to see only call sites / usages.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of references to display. Default 100.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_findOverrides
languageModelTools.modelDescription: For a C/C++ virtual method, returns every concrete implementation across the codebase using the tree-sitter inheritance closure built during workspace indexing. Use BEFORE modifying a virtual method's signature, behavior contract, or visibility — every `high`-confidence override returned must be updated in lockstep; `medium`/`low` results should be sanity-checked. Closes the virtual-dispatch blind spot that text grep cannot solve. Required: `symbolName` (the method name).

filePath.description: Optional path to a C/C++ file. Accepted for backwards-compatibility; the resolver searches the workspace inheritance closure.

symbolName.description: Name of the virtual method (e.g. 'doWork', 'bar'). Required.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of overrides to display. Default 100.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_callHierarchy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: For a C/C++ function, returns who calls it (incoming) and/or what it calls (outgoing) using Hive Mind's tree-sitter call-edge index. Supports `depth: 2` to trace two levels in a single call (e.g. callers-of-callers). Use this when tracing bugs across function boundaries or before changing a function's signature/contract to understand the upstream and downstream blast radius. Required: `symbolName` (or qualified name like `MyClass::handle`).

filePath.description: Optional path to a C/C++ file. Accepted for backwards-compatibility; the resolver searches the workspace function index.

direction.description: incoming = who calls this | outgoing = what this calls | both (default).

depth.description: How many levels deep to trace (1 or 2). Default 1. Use 2 sparingly — output grows quickly.

maxPerLevel.description: Cap on number of children at each level. Default 25.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_typeHierarchy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: For a C/C++ class, struct, or interface, returns its supertypes (base classes) and/or subtypes (derived classes) using Hive Mind's tree-sitter inheritance index. Handles multiple inheritance. Use BEFORE modifying any base class — every subtype returned may be affected. Combine with `hivemind_findOverrides` on individual virtual methods to find concrete implementations to update. Required: `symbolName` (the class/struct/interface name).

filePath.description: Optional path to a C/C++ file. Accepted for backwards-compatibility; the resolver searches the workspace inheritance closure.

symbolName.description: Name of the class, struct, or interface (e.g. 'IFoo', 'BaseWidget'). Required.

depth.description: How many levels deep to trace (1, 2, or 3). Default 2.

maxPerLevel.description: Cap on number of children at each level. Default 25.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_macroExpand
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs the C/C++ preprocessor (`cl.exe /E` on Windows, `g++ -E` on Linux/Mac, with clang as a fallback) and returns the actual preprocessor expansion of a specific source line. Flag source priority: compile_commands.json entry → active project profile (e.g. the built-in Sage X3 profile) → workspace heuristics. Use this when you need to know what `FCIMPL3(MyMethod, ...)` (or any nested/conditional macro) ACTUALLY becomes in this specific translation unit — `hivemind_findMacro` only shows the #define text and cannot account for #ifdef branches, -D flags, or recursive expansion. Required: `filePath` (must be a .cpp/.cc/.cxx — not a header), `line` (1-indexed). Optional: `contextLines` (default 0; ±N lines around `line`).

filePath.description: Path to a C/C++ source file (.cpp/.cc/.cxx/.c). Headers are NOT supported — call this on a TU that #includes the header instead.

contextLines.description: Include ±N lines of expansion around the target line. Default 0. Cap 20.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the clang invocation. Default 30000.

languageModelTools.name: hivemind_buildSubset
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs syntax-only compilation (`cl.exe /Zs` on Windows, `g++ -fsyntax-only` on Linux/Mac, with clang as a fallback) on every translation unit in the impact set of a seed file. Flag source priority: each TU's compile_commands.json entry → active project profile (e.g. the built-in Sage X3 profile). This is the cheapest way to answer 'did I break the build?' after editing a header — no codegen, no linking, no PDB locking. Returns pass/fail per TU plus compiler diagnostics. Required: `filePath` (the changed file). Optional: `maxTUs` (default 50, cap 500), `depth`, `perFileTimeoutMs`, `totalBudgetMs`, `parallelism`, `explicitTUs` (skip impact walk; compile exactly these TUs).

filePath.description: Seed file (the one changed). Reverse-dependency walk starts here.

maxTUs.description: Cap on number of translation units to compile. Default 50. Above 200 is unwise on large repos.

perFileTimeoutMs.description: Per-TU compiler timeout in milliseconds. Default 60000.

totalBudgetMs.description: Total wallclock budget in milliseconds. Default 300000 (5 min).

parallelism.description: Max concurrent compiler processes. Default min(8, CPU count).

explicitTUs.description: Optional explicit list of TUs to compile. Skips the impact-graph walk; only these files are checked.
